blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: Tell me about the group. What did you observe at the bonfire beyond what you told me last night.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: Six people. Easy body language overall — the ease of long familiarity. But even in comfortable groups there are fault lines. People who drift to each other, people who hold a certain distance.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: Subgroups. Tensions that don't announce themselves. There's always something underneath in a group that's known each other that long. Enough history to make the present complicated.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: The man being watched — the one who died — he was at the edge. Not excluded, but separate. The body language of someone with something on his mind that he hadn't shared. The group wasn't aware of it. They were reading him as present. He wasn't entirely present.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: His friends said he seemed off this weekend. One of them said he seemed like someone hearing bad news in an empty room.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: The quiet one. Short answers, precise ones.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: Nothing I can quantify yet. Impressions. I'd rather give you evidence than impressions.
